communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: March 11 DA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please see enclosed PFD

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Maxine Doogan

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: March 11 DA
communications.communication:
A determination whether there are disclosable public records in the possession of this agency cannot be made within 10 days.  Up to 14 days additional time is required pursuant to Government Code section 7922.535(b) because the material to be checked is in other establishments separate from the office processing the request and because there is a need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request.  We will respond by April 6, 2026.

[cid:image001.png@01DCBAB6.95B4CAA0]
Michael Blazina | Assistant District Attorney
Sacramento County District Attorney's Office
901 G Street | Sacramento, CA  95814

files.source: Sacramento County District Attorney's Office, Sacramento County, CA

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: March 11 DA
communications.communication:
Thanks so much
Maxine Doogan

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: March 11 DA
communications.communication:
Ms. Doogan –

The following is in response to your requests made on March 11, 2026.

With regards to request #1, our office does not have a document or index that contains a list of the cases or documents you are specifically seeking.  The CPRA applies to existing records and does not require a public agency to create a record that does not exist.  (Gov’t Code §§ 7920.530, 7920.545.)  To determine this information would require a hand search of tens of thousands of files.  This would require us to search an enormous volume of data and is objectionable as unduly burdensome, something not required under the CPRA.  (See Gov’t Code § 7922.000; County of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 588, at 600-60; see also American Civil Liberties Union v. Deukmejian (1982) 32 Cal.3d 440, at 452-454.)

That being said, we are able to track cases filed from our office computerized case management system based on which specific criminal code sections are charged.  We can provide publicly available information from when we file a charging document, specifically, the docket number and the charges.  However, because we do not already have a report with the requested information, under the CPRA, the requester is responsible for paying the costs associated with preparing the computer inquiry to retrieve the data.  (Gov’t Code § 7922.575(b).)  It is estimated it will take one (1) hour of an IT Analyst II’s time at a cost of $103.81 to retrieve the lists of cases in which violations of the above noted Penal Code sections were charged for the time frame indicated.  You would be responsible for paying that cost before we proceed.  After the data is assembled, if it took less computer programming time than had been estimated, you would receive a refund; if it took more time, then you would be required to pay the balance before receiving the data.

As for the additional information you seek regarding the generated list of cases, that information is exempt from disclosure as part of our investigatory files and would not be provided.  Government Code section 7923.600(a) exempts from CPRA disclosure records that are part of investigatory files compiled by local agencies for law enforcement purposes, which includes district attorneys’ offices.  (See Rivero v. Superior Court (1997) 54 Cal.App.4th 1048; Rackaucas v. Superior Court (2012) 104 Cal.App.4th 169; Fagan v. Superior Court (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 607; County of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1099.)  This exemption applies not only to documents generated by the agency, but also to records gathered from other sources.  (See Haynie v. Superior Court (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1061.)  This exemption continues to apply even if the case is complete, and the investigation is closed.  (See Rivero v. Superior Court (1997) 54 Cal.App.4th 1048; Williams v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 337.)

If you wish to proceed with your request for a list of cases with the information we have indicated we can provide, please remit a check in the amount of $103.81 made out to the Sacramento County District Attorney’s Office.

With regards to requests #2 and #3, we can provide you now the attached documents.  However, additional time is required pursuant to Government Code section 7922.535(b) because the material to be checked is in other establishments separate from the office processing the request and because there is a need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request.  We will respond further on these requests by April 20, 2026.

Sincerely,
Michael Blazina


[cid:image001.png@01DCC5D7.A834DEF0]
Michael Blazina | Assistant District Attorney
Sacramento County District Attorney's Office
901 G Street | Sacramento, CA  95814

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: 12-18-2025 Evanston Border Patrol Attack
communications.communication:
To the FOIA Officer of Evanston Police Department,

This request is made pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act for records sufficient to document any involvement actions observations communications or presence of Evanston Police Department personnel in connection with federal enforcement activity conducted by United States Customs and Border Protection within Evanston.

Incident date:
December 17 2025

Locations:
Mobil gas station at 1950 Green Bay Road Evanston Illinois
Home Depot at 2201 Oakton Street Evanston Illinois

Description:
Public statements issued by the City of Evanston and the Evanston Police Department acknowledge that Evanston Police responded to multiple incidents involving Customs and Border Protection (CBP) activity and that officers were present. Public reporting further describes Evanston Police officers supervisors and command staff on scene directing traffic interacting with federal agents and remaining present during detentions and community response. This request seeks records sufficient to document the actual scope and nature of Evanston Police Department involvement.

Specifically, I seek:

1) All unedited body worn camera recordings and associated audio captured by any Evanston Police Department officer present at the above locations for the full duration of their presence
2) Any dash camera, in vehicle Fleet camera, or similar recordings from Evanston Police Department vehicles present at or responding to the above locations
3) All dispatch records, computer aided dispatch logs, call for service records, and associated audio including any 911 calls related to these incidents
4) All records generated or received by the Evanston Police Department in connection with these incidents including incident reports supplemental reports investigative or informational reports supervisor notes approval or command level documents photographs videos attachments officer narrative supplements and any written or electronic communications referencing Customs and Border Protection or federal agents

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the case of any applicable fees, it is requested that an estimate of the total charges be provided prior to fulfilling this request. The preferred method of receipt is through electronic delivery.

Your assistance and cooperation in this matter are greatly appreciated. A response within 5 business days, as the statute requires, is anticipated.

With thanks,

David S. (he/him/his), Lucy Parsons Labs

communications.subject: Your City of Evanston public records request #25-4429 has been closed.
communications.communication:
City of Evanston ************************************************************************ Record request #25-4429 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Denial of Request Your Request is hereby Denied in its entirety. The reason(s) for denial are stated below: 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a) and 50 ILCS 706/10-20(b) In order for body-worn camera footage to be given under the act, the person must be the subject of the video, or a qualifying event must occur    You have a right to appeal this decision within 60 days to Leah Bartelt, Public Access Counselor, Office of the Illinois Attorney General, 500 South 2nd Street, Springfield, Illinois 62701, (phone number 1-877-299-3642 email public.access@ilag.gov) or to seek judicial review under Section 11 of FOIA, 5 ILCS 140/11. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Evanston.

Technical support: See our help page

 Jennifer Levi from Evanston Police Dept. - IL (https://evanstonpdil.evidence.com) has sent you a link to download the case FOIA 25-4429 on Evidence.com. communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: 321D-IR-A7788
communications.communication:
Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on "Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request" on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@ic.fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI's FOIPA Program is searching the FBI's indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 134 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 523 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,335 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 1,875 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
Record/Information Dissemination Section (RIDS) FBI-Information Management Division
170 Marcel Drive, Winchester, VA 22602-4843
Direct: (540) 868-4593
Fax: (540) 868-4391
Questions E-mail: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

title: RAW RIPA Data (San Fernando Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: RAW RIPA Data (San Fernando Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, 

I am requesting your department's state mandated RIPA stop data from 2020 to today's date. Please upload in .csv or Excel format. No PDFs. 

Thank you

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: Mark Middleton Files - Jeffrey Epstein Folder (Box 2, OA #4504)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Mark Middleton Files - Jeffrey Epstein Folder (Box 2, OA #4504)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records contained in the folder titled "JEFFREY EPSTEIN" within the Mark Middleton files, Box 2 (OA #4504, NARA #3164). This folder is documented in the STAIRS/VS Aquarius database printout (Document Number 101694, dated February 28, 1995), which was released under FOIA case LPWJC_2008-0825-F.

I also request any related finding aids, withdrawal sheets, or indices describing the contents of this folder, as well as any other folders within the 6 Middleton boxes (OA
  #4503, 4504, 4505, 4509, 4510, 4511) that reference Jeffrey Epstein.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Mark Middleton Files - Jeffrey Epstein Folder (Box 2, OA #4504)
communications.communication:

communications.subject: re: Your FOIA request
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Galaxy,

Attached is the FOIA response letter regarding your request 2026-0425-F.
The requested file was already processed under a previous FOIA request and
consists of three pages.

Archives Technician Jason Kaplan will email the digitized scans to you this
afternoon or Monday morning from our reference address,
clinton.library@nara.gov.

If you have any questions, please let me know.

Sincerely,

Racheal Carter-Ragan
FOIA Coordinator
William J. Clinton Presidential Library
1200 President Clinton Avenue
Little Rock, AR 72201

communications.subject: Re:  Your request for scans from 2019-0820-F Segment 1 Mark Middleton
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Galaxy,

Thank you for contacting the William J. Clinton Presidential Library and 
Museum, a part of the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA). 
Our FOIA Coordinator, Ms. Racheal Carter-Ragan, forwarded your request for 
scans of the folder entitled "[Alpha Files] - Jeffrey Epstein [OA/ID 4504]" 
located in Box 4 of 2019-0820-F Segment 1 Mark Middleton 
<https://clinton.presidentiallibraries.us/items/show/118574>. I have 
attached the PDF of the documents that were in this folder to this email. 
As it is both under 15 pages *and* was previously scanned for another 
reproduction request, there is no charge for these documents. Please accept 
them with our compliments. Also note that these were all of the documents 
that were located in that folder.

Sincerely,

Jason Kaplan

Archives Technician

William J. Clinton Presidential Library

1200 President Clinton Avenue

Little Rock, AR 72201

clinton.library@nara.gov

501-244-2877

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Compensation Agreement for Anduril Arsenal-1
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

The Compensation Agreement between the tenant, developer, and authority and Teays Valley Local Schools which provides for the School District to receive certain compensation in exchange for the tax revenue foregone by the School District as a result of the Community Reinvestment Area Exemptions granted by the Pickaway County Agreement for the Anduril Arsenal-1 Hyperscale Manufacturing Facility project.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Taylor Barnes

communications.subject: RE: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Compensation Agreement for Anduril Arsenal-1
communications.communication:
Thank you very much Trey, I really appreciate your providing me with this.

best

Taylor Barnes

communications.subject: RE: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Compensation Agreement for Anduril Arsenal-1
communications.communication:
Hi Trey,

I really appreciate your providing me with this agreement. May I ask two follow up questions?

One, pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, may I have a copy of the semi-annual PILOT Statement produced between January 1- March 1 of this year that specifies that the amount due for the first half of the annual Project Thor PILOT payment to the Teays Valley Local School District? If Teays Valley produced any objections to the calculation, could you kindly provide me with the objection?

Also, may I ask if you all have estimated what your revenue loss will be over 30 years due to the compensation agreement equaling 30% of the portion of real property taxes Teays Valley would have received had the CRA Exemption not been in place for the Arsenal-1 buildings? Ie, what amount does Teays Valley estimate that the full 100% would have been?

I really appreciate the consideration -- thank you very much. 

best

Taylor Barnes

communications.subject: RE: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Compensation Agreement for Anduril Arsenal-1
communications.communication:
Good morning,



Please find the responses to the follow-up questions below.



One, pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, may I have a copy of the
semi-annual PILOT Statement produced between January 1- March 1 of this
year that specifies that the amount due for the first half of the annual
Project Thor PILOT payment to the Teays Valley Local School District? If
Teays Valley produced any objections to the calculation, could you kindly
provide me with the objection?

The school district has not yet received the semi-annual PILOT statement
from the County for the first half of this year. At this time we do not
have any responsive records to share regarding the statement itself or any
potential objections to the PILOT calculations.



Also, may I ask if you all have estimated what your revenue loss will be
over 30 years due to the compensation agreement equaling 30% of the portion
of real property taxes Teays Valley would have received had the CRA
Exemption not been in place for the Arsenal-1 buildings? Ie, what amount
does Teays Valley estimate that the full 100% would have been?

The school district does not maintain any records calculating the
anticipated revenue loss. For your convenience I have attached a record of
the Press Release the district made that speaks to anticipated revenues.

Sincerely,



Trey Fausnaugh

Teays Valley Local School District

385 Viking Way

Ashville, Ohio 43103

*tfausnaugh@tvsd.us <tfausnaugh@tvsd.us>*

Phone: 740.983.5012

best,

Taylor Barnes

title: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Merced County Registrar of Voters)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Merced County Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in your County in that year. In case it is helpful to see an example of the type of data I am requesting from your county, Alameda County has these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

Please let me know if you need any further clarification on the type of data that I am requesting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Alger County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Alger County)
communications.communication:
Dear Alger County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Alger County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Inmate Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the applicable Public Records Act / Freedom of Information laws, I respectfully request copies of any and all inmate disciplinary records, incident reports, rule violation reports, sanctions, or related documentation concerning Anna Henson with DOB 1/18/1996 during her period of incarceration from January 5, 2026 through January 24, 2026.

This request includes, but is not limited to:

Disciplinary charges or infractions

Incident or misconduct reports

Findings, determinations, or outcomes of any disciplinary proceedings

Sanctions imposed or recommended

Related logs or summaries referencing disciplinary matters

If no responsive records exist, please confirm that in writing.

If any portion of this request is denied or redacted, please identify the specific statutory basis for the denial or redaction and release any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of the records.

Thank you for your assistance. Please feel free to contact me if you require clarification or additional information to process this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Frank Freeman.

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff Detention (Oswego County Sheriffs Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

- The most recent contract, including any amendments and modifications, between your office and U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and/ or the U.S. Marshals for the purpose of allowing ICE to utilize bed space in your jail.
- Records relating annual inspection of the facility conducted in 2023, 2024, and 2025 by the U.S. Department Homeland Security. These records should include the full annual inspection report and any documents or communications regarding the inspection.
- Records relating any other local, state, or federal inspections of the facility conducted in 2023, 2024, and 2025.
- Any ICE Enforcement and Removal Operations Facility Significant Incident Summary (SIS) produced between January 1, 2023, and the date this request is processed.

Since January 1, 2023:
- Communications, including electronic communications and attached documents, between your department and ICE and/or the U.S. Marshals related to the contract. This should include any communications and documents relating to requests, approvals, or denials of waivers of contract provisions.
- Billings, invoices, and records of payment made pursuant to the contract.
- Monthly records on the average daily population and average length of detention of people detained in ICE custody at the jail.
- Complaints or grievances filed by people detained in ICE custody at the jail.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Julia Rock

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: UC Berkeley Public Records Log
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the 2025 log of requests made to UC Berkeley.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Mitch Poran

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: Chauntae Davies investigatory records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Chauntae Davies, an alleged victim of Jeffrey Epstein, has stated that she cooperated with the New Mexico Attorney General's Office starting in 2019. Please provide any records related to Chauntae Davies' engagement with the Office beginning January 1, 2019 to January 1, 2022. Please include any records related to Chauntae Davies and Jeffrey Epstein, Ghislaine Maxwell, and the Zorro Ranch, located near Stanley, New Mexico.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Tracey

communications.subject: Your New Mexico Department of Justice public records request #25-498 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
New Mexico Department of Justice ************************************************************************ Your record request #25-498 has been submitted successfully. This is an automated message. Thank you for submitting your request to the New Mexico Department of Justice. We will review your request and begin processing it. Typically, you will receive two communications from us, one within three business days and the second within 15 days. If we need additional time to process your request, we will let you know in our 15 day communication. If you have any documents relevant to your records request, please upload them or provide copies to us to assist with our searches. Please also let us know if you are looking for an exact document within our records so that we may provide it to you more quickly. We strive to complete requests as timely and completely as we can and your assistance will help us in this process. If you need to contact us, you may reply to this message. ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact New Mexico Department of Justice.

New Mexico Department of Justice ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-498: Dear Mr. Tracey: Our office received your records request for: Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: Chauntae Davies, an alleged victim of Jeffrey Epstein, has stated that she cooperated with the New Mexico Attorney General's Office starting in 2019. Please provide any records related to Chauntae Davies' engagement with the Office beginning January 1, 2019 to January 1, 2022. Please include any records related to Chauntae Davies and Jeffrey Epstein, Ghislaine Maxwell, and the Zorro Ranch, located near Stanley, New Mexico. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. Via the link below, please find an installment of records that are responsive to your request. Please note that, due to the number of records remaining to be reviewed, this request is excessively burdensome at this time, and we need more time to process your request. We will respond to you no later than December 29, 2025. https://share.nmag.gov/url/4xpht3zakjbzpujs Please note that, with regards to this installment, no redactions took place. Any withholdings-in-full are tentative until this request is closed, at which point you will receive a list of exemptions supporting them. I am the official responsible for any denial of your request. Best regards, Carrie Cook IPRA Attorney ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at New Mexico Department of Justice.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Whatcom County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Whatcom County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Sheriff Records Request :: S000120-012926
communications.communication:
Dear Samuel Sinyangwe: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Whatcom County Sheriff's Office. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 1/29/2026 and given the reference number S000120-012926 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2202249/ 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Whatcom County Sheriff's Office

communications.subject: Welcome to the Records Center!
communications.communication:
Dear Samuel Sinyangwe, 
 
Thank you for registering with the Whatcom County Public Records Access Center. (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXYpIX-2B-2BYy4ubctoJ6-2FPWdkaLhjhE-2FDq3hfSKXIB46BpsbH-2FkB1DTaUkNQLJe4Zcong-3D-3D2DJI_19ovHMj8egmJ7EmezBCTH8soTWh7oMnVwJ-2B3w0FAGp0nhTT0gOXhdCarqFJ0M9jn7RDBIVAlb-2FDcgb-2B7FVVDgs71O-2BM5FTrsa69aejgtYZrt2paQYY47ATJV6v9UaIv-2BEGEj30D81IL9go2oR1hYDUPvkjR31oPLJZol6u7UMSOBBaqNMiJcSfurTosCH4yyQYqI2UOBMvCz6X8SZckcOUrZcDCf-2FAiuAnxQuDbxnao-3D)

communications.subject: New Message :: S000120-012926
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: Sheriff Records Request of January 29, 2026, Reference # S000120-012926 Dear Samuel Sinyangwe, The Whatcom County Sheriff's Office received a request from you on January 29, 2026. Your request mentioned: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2202249/ We are currently assembling and researching the requested documents and will respond to your request within 30 business days (See RCW 42.56.520). For questions or additional information, please reply to this email. Sincerely, Sheriff Records

Please find the records you requested from our agency attached (PDR Reference S000120-012926). I am emailing them to you as they did not format correctly when uploaded into the GovQA portal. All responsive records have been provided and this request is now closed.

title: Police disciplinary files (Walla Walla County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Police disciplinary files (Walla Walla County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States.
- Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Moe Clark

communications.subject: Your Walla Walla County public records request #25-634 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
Walla Walla County ************************************************************************ Your record request #25-634 has been submitted successfully. This letter serves as Walla Walla County’s initial five-day letter, pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, to your public records request. After your request is reviewed for content and we determine the department or office in which your request refers to, the County will provide an estimate to provide an initial installment of responsive records. After locating responsive records, the County will determine whether any of the responsive documents are subject to applicable exemptions under the Public Records Act, and to notify any third parties affected by the request. If third party notification is required, twenty (20) business days will be allowed for a response by the third party to those records which specifically pertain to them. As records are reviewed, they may be released on an installment basis. Additionally, the Washington Public Records Act (RCW 42.56.120) and Walla Walla County Resolution 23-201 now allow the County to charge for provision of electronic records. Refer to the hyperlinked documents for the fee schedule for future records installments. ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact Walla Walla County.

title: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request – Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243

Dear Open Records Officer,

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., I respectfully request access to the following public administrative and clerical records maintained by the Office of the Clerk of State Court concerning Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243 in DeKalb County State Court and/or Magistrate Court, as applicable:
1. Deputy Clerk Identities
Names and titles of all deputy clerks or Clerk’s Office personnel who processed, reviewed, docketed, rejected, closed, dismissed, or otherwise handled filings or records in these cases.

2. Digital and Administrative Records
For each filing in the above cases, I request:
Any internal audit logs, access logs, activity histories, or other system-generated records maintained by the Clerk’s Office within any electronic case management, e-filing, or document management system used to receive, review, process, docket, store, or manage filings, identifying all users who accessed or took administrative action on those filings.

Metadata associated with filings and docket entries, including timestamps, user actions, status changes, and internal notes.

Internal clerk review annotations, comments, or administrative correspondence associated with each filing.

Any administrative records generated in the course of processing, docketing, closing, dismissing, or maintaining these case records.

Copies of all filings, docket entries, and case status records sufficient to ensure a complete and accurate administrative record, including any updates or amendments.

3.  Administrative Policies and Training Materials
Written policies, procedures, or training materials governing the processing, docketing, dismissal, closure, and record maintenance of State Court and Magistrate Court matters, including procedures applicable when a defendant does not appear.

4.  Scope and Limitations
This request is limited to administrative and ministerial records and public documentation. It does not seek confidential judicial deliberations, legal analysis, or privileged attorney communications.

If any portion of a responsive record is claimed to be exempt from disclosure, please produce all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions with redactions as permitted by law and identify the specific statutory basis for each redaction or withholding, pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(d).

5. Purpose and System Applicability 
The purpose of this request is to identify the personnel responsible for processing these filings and to obtain a complete and accurate administrative and digital record of how these matters were handled and maintained by the Clerk’s Office.

This request applies to any electronic system or database used by the Clerk’s Office to receive, process, review, manage, or store filings for these cases, regardless of vendor or system name.

6. Format and Fees
If any fees are associated with producing these records, please notify me prior to processing. I request that the records be provided in electronic format, if available.

Thank you for your attention to this request. 

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
Subject: Second Request and Notice of Noncompliance – Georgia Open Records Act

Dear Open Records Officer,

This message follows my Georgia Open Records Act request submitted on 12/27/2025 via the MuckRock Foundation concerning administrative records related to Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243.

As of today, more than three business days have elapsed since the request was received. No response, acknowledgment, production, denial, or notice of extension has been provided.

Under the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A), an agency is required within three business days to produce the requested records, provide a timetable for production, or issue a written denial citing the specific legal exemptions relied upon. A failure to respond constitutes noncompliance with the Act.

Accordingly, this email serves as a restatement of the original request and as notice that the Office is currently out of compliance with GORA. 

Please provide:
	1	The requested records; or
	2	A written response identifying when responsive records will be produced; or
	3	A written denial identifying the specific statutory exemptions relied upon for any records withheld.

If the Office contends that no responsive records exist, please confirm that determination in writing.

I respectfully request that the Office respond within five (5) business days to cure this noncompliance. If the matter is not resolved, I will consider pursuing the remedies available under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-73, including referral to the Georgia Attorney General’s Office or judicial enforcement.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

Subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request – Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243

Dear Open Records Officer,

Thank you for your note forwarding this request. To clarify, this request concerns administrative and clerical records maintained by the Clerk’s Office for Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243. It does not pertain to dispossessory filings, and the Dispossessory Department is not relevant to this request.

Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A), the Clerk’s Office is required within three business days of receipt to:

Produce the requested records,

Provide a timetable for production, or

Issue a written denial citing specific statutory exemptions for any records withheld.

The previous forwarding to another department does not satisfy this statutory obligation. I respectfully request that the Clerk’s Office respond directly with the requested records, a production timeline, or a written denial citing exemptions.

Please provide a response within five (5) business days of this notice to cure the ongoing noncompliance. If the office does not respond substantively within this timeframe, I may pursue the remedies available under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-73, including referral to the Georgia Attorney General or judicial enforcement.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Respectfully,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr. , pro se

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Rodriguez,

I am writing to follow up on my Georgia Open Records Act request, originally submitted on December 27, 2025, regarding Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243, and subsequent correspondence, including my March 17, 2026 follow-up requesting a more definite production timeline and a general description of responsive record categories.

As of today, I have not received a substantive update regarding the status of this request or any information regarding the search, identification, or anticipated production of records. Given the upcoming trial in this matter, scheduled for April 6–8, 2026, it is important to have a clear understanding of the status and availability of administrative and clerical records maintained by the Clerk’s Office.

To ensure compliance with O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq. and to facilitate proper documentation, I respectfully request the following information at your earliest convenience:
	
1	Categories of Responsive Records Identified – A general description of the types of administrative and clerical records your office has identified as responsive to this request.
	
2	Search Scope and Methodology – A description of which offices, departments, electronic systems, and personnel are reviewing records for this request, including the procedures, criteria, or keywords applied in identifying responsive records.
	
3	Records Determination – Clarification regarding any records that may be withheld, partially withheld, or redacted, along with the statutory basis for any such exemptions.
	
4	Updated Production Timeline – A specific estimated timeframe for the production of records identified as responsive, considering the current stage of review.

Providing this information will help ensure the request is processed thoroughly and will allow for alignment with ongoing trial preparation. Documentation of these details is consistent with the transparency objectives of the Georgia Open Records Act and will assist in maintaining a clear record of the Clerk’s Office’s processing of this request.

I respectfully request a response no later than March 31, 2026, so that any available information can be considered in advance of the April 6 trial.

Thank you for your attention to this matter and your continued cooperation.

Sincerely,
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Open Records Request Act - Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243
communications.communication:
Final Demand and Notice of Noncompliance – Georgia Open Records Act Request (Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243) 

Dear Ms. Rodriguez,

This correspondence serves as a final demand regarding my Georgia Open Records Act request originally submitted on December 27, 2025, concerning administrative and clerical records for Case Nos. 21R02895 and 23D06243.

This request remains directed to the DeKalb County State Court Clerk’s Office as the legal custodian of the requested records.

As you are aware, the Georgia Open Records Act requires that an agency, within three (3) business days of receipt, either produce responsive records, provide a specific timetable for production, or issue a written denial identifying the legal basis for withholding records. See O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A).

To date, more than three months have elapsed. No records have been produced, no legally sufficient denial has been issued, and no definite production schedule has been provided. Repeated statements indicating that “10–14 business days” are needed, without actual production or firm dates, do not satisfy the requirements of the Act.

Additionally, this request concerns administrative and clerical records maintained by the Clerk’s Office and is not limited to, nor properly routed through, the Dispossessory Department. Continued routing of this request to an unrelated division has contributed to delay and noncompliance.

Please also be advised that these records are directly relevant to ongoing litigation involving the handling, processing, and administrative management of the referenced cases. Accordingly, you are under a duty to preserve all responsive records, including but not limited to electronic records, audit logs, metadata, internal communications, and any system-generated data reflecting access or activity.

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby demand that within three (3) business days you provide:
Immediate production of all responsive records currently available;
A definite, good-faith production schedule with specific calendar dates for any remaining records; or
A written denial identifying each withheld record or category and the specific statutory exemption relied upon.

Failure to comply will result in pursuit of all available remedies under the Georgia Open Records Act, including enforcement proceedings, as well as appropriate use of this record of noncompliance in related litigation.

I remain willing to work cooperatively to facilitate prompt resolution of this matter.

Sincerely, 

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Concord School District/SAU8)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: Flock Safety / ALPR Surveillance Records – Town of East Fishkill

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: FOIL Request – Flock Safety / ALPR Surveillance Records – Town of East Fishkill
communications.communication:
Dear Records Custodian,

Under the provisions of the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL), N.Y. Public Officers Law §§ 84–90, I hereby request access to and copies of records held by the Town of East Fishkill, Dutchess County, relating to the planning, proposal, procurement, authorization, or attempted deployment of Flock Safety surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems.

This request is made in the public interest to better understand the use, consideration, or implementation of surveillance technologies by local government entities, including decision-making processes, vendor relationships, and potential impacts on public privacy.

The purpose of this request is to determine the origin, authorization process, procurement activity, proposed locations, and communications surrounding any surveillance camera system, including identifying which individual, agency, or vendor first initiated discussions or planning for such a program within the Town of East Fishkill.

Timeframe: January 1, 2023 – Present, including any earlier records referencing or relating to Flock Safety, automated license plate readers, or surveillance cameras within the Town of East Fishkill.

Records Requested

For the purpose of this request, the records I seek include, but are not limited to the following:

1. Contracts and Agreements

All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, purchase orders, vendor agreements, procurement documents, or proposals involving the Town of East Fishkill and Flock Safety or any related entities concerning surveillance cameras, automated license plate readers, or related monitoring technology.

This includes draft contracts, executed agreements, amendments, pilot program agreements, trial program documentation, cancellation notices, procurement justifications, and contract termination records.

Legal Basis: Contracts involving public funds or public infrastructure are public records subject to disclosure under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87.

2. Records Identifying Project Initiation

All records reflecting the initial introduction, proposal, solicitation, or outreach regarding Flock Safety cameras or ALPR systems, including records identifying:

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system  
• the first communications between the Town and Flock Safety  
• vendor outreach to town officials  
• internal discussions about introducing the technology  
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors  

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Town Supervisor  
• Town Board members  
• Town of East Fishkill Police Department personnel  
• Town administrative staff  
• Town legal counsel  
• Highway or Building Department personnel  
• other municipal offices  

that discuss:

• Flock Safety  
• automated license plate readers  
• surveillance cameras  
• camera deployments  
• vendor procurement or negotiations  
• surveillance technology adoption  

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the Town of East Fishkill and:

• representatives of Flock Safety  
• technology vendors or consultants  
• Dutchess County officials  
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies  
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers  

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks  
• vendor proposals  
• demonstration scheduling emails  
• meeting invitations or call summaries  
• webinar invitations  
• follow-up communications  

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the Town of East Fishkill.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements  
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps  
• deployment plans  
• proposed installation locations or intersections  
• roadway monitoring points  
• entry or exit routes to the town  
• communications discussing site selection  
• vendor recommendations for placement  
• field surveys or site inspections  
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement  

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans  
• camera location maps  
• infrastructure assessments  
• equipment specifications  
• installation schedules  
• operational capability descriptions  
• technology architecture documents  

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices  
• payment records  
• purchase authorizations  
• budget allocations  
• grant applications  
• cost analyses  
• procurement justifications  
• financial approvals  

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from Town Board meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where surveillance camera programs or Flock Safety systems were discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to surveillance camera programs or procurement processes.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data  
• policies governing license plate reader searches  
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks  
• data retention policies  
• privacy or civil liberties assessments  

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information  
• CC and BCC fields  
• full email header routing data  
• timestamps for sending and receipt  
• message ID fields  
• attachment metadata  
• document creation dates  
• last modified dates  
• author fields  
• file path information where available  

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter  
Independent Investigative Journalist

files.title: Fee_Waiver_Letter_copy.pdf
files.source: Jordan Lassiter

Legal Basis: Contracts involving public funds or public infrastructure are public records subject to disclosure under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87.

2. Records Identifying Project Initiation

All records reflecting the initial introduction, proposal, solicitation, or outreach regarding Flock Safety cameras or ALPR systems, including records identifying:

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system
• the first communications between the Town and Flock Safety
• vendor outreach to town officials
• internal discussions about introducing the technology
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Town Supervisor
• Town Board members
• Town of East Fishkill Police Department personnel
• Town administrative staff
• Town legal counsel
• Highway or Building Department personnel
• other municipal offices

that discuss:

• Flock Safety
• automated license plate readers
• surveillance cameras
• camera deployments
• vendor procurement or negotiations
• surveillance technology adoption

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the Town of East Fishkill and:

• representatives of Flock Safety
• technology vendors or consultants
• Dutchess County officials
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks
• vendor proposals
• demonstration scheduling emails
• meeting invitations or call summaries
• webinar invitations
• follow-up communications

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the Town of East Fishkill.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps
• deployment plans
• proposed installation locations or intersections
• roadway monitoring points
• entry or exit routes to the town
• communications discussing site selection
• vendor recommendations for placement
• field surveys or site inspections
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans
• camera location maps
• infrastructure assessments
• equipment specifications
• installation schedules
• operational capability descriptions
• technology architecture documents

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices
• payment records
• purchase authorizations
• budget allocations
• grant applications
• cost analyses
• procurement justifications
• financial approvals

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from Town Board meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where surveillance camera programs or Flock Safety systems were discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to surveillance camera programs or procurement processes.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data
• policies governing license plate reader searches
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks
• data retention policies
• privacy or civil liberties assessments

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information
• CC and BCC fields
• full email header routing data
• timestamps for sending and receipt
• message ID fields
• attachment metadata
• document creation dates
• last modified dates
• author fields
• file path information where available

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter
Independent Investigative Journalist

title: Request to Navy for 2018 budget report of Public Affairs Office

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Navy for 2019 budget report of Public Affairs Office
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The annual budget report for the Navy's Public Affairs Office for the year 2018.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

Good morning Mr. Carollo, 

This is to acknowledge the receipt of your request and to let you know that I am the analyst assigned for processing.  If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to me via email. 

Please be aware that, whenever a FOIA requester has not received a determination within 20 working days from the date a request is perfected, they are entitled to challenge the delay.  To do this, you may submit an appeal within the SecureRelease online portal (www.securerelease.us) or by writing to the Department of the Navy, Office of the Judge Advocate General (Code 14), 1322 Patterson Ave SE, Suite 3000, Washington Navy Yard, DC 20374-5066.  If you prefer, you can also submit an appeal via email to Navy-OJAG-C14-FOIA-PA-ADMINAPPEALS@us.navy.mil.  Your appeal, if any, should include a statement indicating why you believe it should be granted.  It must be postmarked or timestamped within 90 calendar days from the date of this letter. 

Requesters also have the right to seek assistance and/or dispute resolution services from the Department of the Navy FOIA Public Liaison, Mr. Christopher Julka, at Christopher.a.julka.civ@us.navy.mil or (703) 697-0031, and may also contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) for assistance and/or dispute resolution at ogis@nara.gov or (877) 684-6448. 

Thank you for your patience as we work to complete the processing of your request.  Hope you have a great day.
