communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: P164004-040626
communications.communication:
Dear LPL: 
 
Thank you for your interest in information from the Chicago Police Department. Your FOIA request has been received and is being processed. Your FOIA Center reference number for tracking purposes is: P164004-040626 
You have requested the following records: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

All emails received by any CPD email address from the sender, p.vitaioli@comcast.net, scope-limited to Jan 1, 2020 through the present date. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

LPL 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2259376/ 
Chicago Police Department (CPD) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq.  If further time is needed to assemble and copy all documents responsive to your FOIA request, we will contact you with a time estimate and a request to extend. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the Chicago FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial Police FOIA Request :: P164004-040626
communications.communication:
--- --- 

04/14/2026 
HICAGO OLICE EPARTMENT 
CITY OF CHICAGO 
LPL LPL 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR208317 263 Huntington Ave 
Boston MA 02115 
RE: FOIA REQUEST of April 06, 2026, Reference # P164004-040626 
Dear LPL: 
The Chicago Police Department is in receipt of your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request. On April 06, 2026, the following request was received. To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

All emails received by any CPD email address from the sender, p.vitaioli@comcast.net, scope-limited to Jan 1, 2020 through the present date. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

LPL 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2259376/ 
Under the Freedom of Information Act, a public body may extend the time to respond to a FOIA request by up to 5 business days for a limited number of reasons. 5 ILCS 140/3(e). We are extending the time to respond to your request by 5 business days for the following reason(s): 
The request may require the collection of a substantial number of specified records. 
If you have any questions, please contact our office at 312-745-5308 or at the following address: Chicago Police Department Attention: Freedom of Information Officer Office of Legal Affairs, Unit 114 3510 S. Michigan Ave. Chicago, IL 60653 
Sincerely, AUDREY SHULRUFF 
Freedom of Information Officer 
Chicago Police Department

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Flock Sharing Settings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am resubmitting this request to obtain the most current versions of the requested records and to determine whether any changes have occurred since my prior request. 

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act (O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq.), I request the following records related to the department’s Flock Safety system, current as of the date this request is fulfilled: 

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system: 
1. A list of network share settings that include: 
1a. Flock networks shared with me 
1b. Networks that I’m sharing 

Please provide these records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (e.g., CSV or spreadsheet files where applicable).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

I am resubmitting this request to obtain the most current versions of the requested records and to determine whether any changes have occurred since my prior request. 

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act (O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq.), I request the following records related to the department’s Flock Safety system, current as of the date this request is fulfilled: 

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system: 
1. A list of network share settings that include: 
1a. Flock networks shared with me 
1b. Networks that I’m sharing 

Please provide these records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (e.g., CSV or spreadsheet files where applicable). 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Jason Hunyar 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2264710/ 
 
Thank you for using the Dunwoody Open Records Center. Eric Shealy Records Clerk Dunwoody Open Records Center  City of Dunwoody 4800 Ashford Dunwoody Road Dunwoody, Georgia 30338 (P) 678-382-6716
